title: 6529reviewbot release operations map
description: Public-safe map of the recurring checks, private operator evidence overlays, and release bundle commands used while preparing dogfood, production cutover, and community releases.

phases.title: Local quality gates
phases.when: Run on every bot change before opening or updating a pull request.
phases.boundary: Public-safe. These commands should not require provider keys, AWS credentials, GitHub App secrets, private payloads, or live deployment identifiers.

tools.purpose: Validate CommonJS syntax, package metadata, repository config examples, and newline hygiene.
tools.publicOutput: Safe to paste when no local paths from failures are included.

tools.purpose: Verify local Markdown links across tracked and non-ignored untracked docs.
tools.publicOutput: Safe after reviewing any failure paths.

tools.purpose: Verify every tracked docs Markdown file is linked from the canonical docs index.
Verify durable manager memory keeps core sections, latest merged PR state, validation status, release-check wiring, smoke tests, and public docs synchronized.
Verify public MIT license metadata, community files, issue templates, and root README governance links.
Verify CODEOWNERS default review ownership, high-risk path coverage, repository ruleset guidance, and governance docs stay synchronized.
Verify broad community-release gates, evidence references, release readiness guidance, and release docs stay synchronized.
Verify main branch protection, required PR checks, release tag ruleset guidance, dry-run tag planning, and operator-owned live ruleset evidence guidance stay synchronized.
Verify the installation guide keeps the conservative central App dogfood path, GitHub App validation commands, runtime defaults, command-only target posture, and rollback controls synchronized.
Verify the production deployment runbook keeps GitHub App registration, central runtime, worker, 6529.io wiring, verification, and rollback guidance synchronized.

Verify the configuration reference keeps central App env, provider defaults, budget controls, worker dispatch, usage/admin APIs, admin auth, alerting, review limits, env templates, and source parser anchors synchronized.
Verify AWS IAM/OIDC examples stay least-privilege, placeholder-only, scoped to the bot repository or protected environment, and linked from production cutover evidence.

Verify the security model and checklist keep first-principles trust boundaries, prompt/path/metadata safety, fail-closed controls, diagnostic redaction, admin/AWS/alert boundaries, and source anchors synchronized.
Verify the pre-v1 compatibility policy, release notes, release process, reusable workflow guidance, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify public docs distinguish local validation from operator-owned production, dashboard, alert, dogfood, security-review, and cutover evidence.
Verify the operations runbook keeps routine checks and triage paths for replay, spend, ledgers, workers, dashboards, and bot comments synchronized.
Verify weekly Dependabot coverage for npm and GitHub Actions dependencies.
Verify the central App server Dockerfile and .dockerignore keep the runtime image minimal, non-root, health-checked, and free of private repo artifacts.
Verify the dry-run container publish plan keeps clean-main, image contract, build/push/scan command, and private evidence guidance synchronized.
Verify public comment-command docs stay synchronized with the parser and review-kind constants.
Verify review-kind constants stay synchronized with worker bins, central workflow dispatch options, reusable workflow defaults, and shell routing.
Verify review context path safety, trusted metadata handling, prompt hygiene, hard caps, and source-boundary docs stay synchronized.
Verify review-kind prompt configs, bin entrypoints, package scripts, and review workflow docs stay synchronized.
Verify generated public PR comment headings, hidden markers, review labels, verdict lines, and budget-skip wording stay synchronized with the public comment-format docs.
Verify trusted-actor admission defaults, repo visibility modes, draft handling, trusted permission levels, public examples, and admission docs stay synchronized.
Verify repository config remains a narrowing layer for lanes, max jobs, admission, budget caps, default cost, and base-ref loading.
Verify worker adapter modes, GitHub dispatch fields, local worker env, redacted diagnostics, workflow template inputs, and worker docs stay synchronized.
Verify worker capacity and backpressure guidance keeps starting caps, scale-up rules, stuck-job triage, provider limits, alert evidence, and release blockers synchronized.
Verify private admin auth modes, shared-secret behavior, HMAC headers, TTL and role checks, 6529.io bridge docs, and public env templates stay synchronized.
Verify usage/admin API routes, server defaults, OpenAPI paths, 6529.io client methods, env templates, and docs stay synchronized.
Verify admin snapshot check names, default policy, warning posture, redaction behavior, CLI flags, and docs stay synchronized.
Verify sanitized support bundle safe env keys, presence-only secret keys, local path redaction, CLI flags, and docs stay synchronized.
Verify public support and incident playbooks keep reporting boundaries, maintainer triage, containment, recovery, and public follow-up guidance synchronized.
Verify shared diagnostic redaction for bearer tokens, GitHub tokens, provider keys, alert webhooks, AWS access-key ids, private keys, error lines, and diagnostic tails.
Verify model-catalog defaults stay synchronized with reusable workflow fallbacks, provider-default docs, and conservative starter lanes.
Verify model pricing guidance keeps price-file shape, source-checked evidence, stale/zero overrides, apply behavior, and estimation semantics synchronized.
Verify model price coverage behavior keeps catalog default provider/model lane coverage, required input/output rates, freshness, zero-placeholder, and placeholder-source checks synchronized.
Verify supported provider constants stay synchronized with model catalog providers, preflight key requirements, workflow dispatch options, and provider docs.
Verify Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter adapter request shapes, option gating, usage normalization, error redaction, and provider docs stay synchronized.

Verify usage, job, and run-control ledgers normalize custom metadata before persistence and that usage API/schema boundaries do not expose prompts, diffs, provider payloads, webhook payloads, credentials, or provider request identifiers.
Verify canonical budget scopes stay synchronized with central policy validation, ledger schema constraints, public docs, and dogfood examples.
Verify budget policy guidance keeps policy-file shape, scope semantics, dry-run/apply behavior, admission precedence, fail-closed posture, and review requirements synchronized.
Verify run-control concurrency scopes stay synchronized with budget scopes, env parsing, claim SQL, docs, and env examples.
Verify scheduled spend-spike alert dimensions stay synchronized across alert defaults, env parsing, docs, and env examples.
Verify scheduled alert delivery modes stay synchronized across notifier constants, env parsing, docs, and env examples.
Verify the scheduled alert runner keeps no-provider behavior, private delivery routing, dogfood evidence, payload privacy, and central-workflow posture synchronized.

Verify the dry-run production alert delivery plan keeps reviewed delivery mode, private channel label, dry-run alert evaluation, admin status verification, cutover evidence, and release notes synchronized.
Scan public docs, config, workflows, templates, and manager memory for live-looking secrets or cloud identifiers.
tools.publicOutput: Safe; findings redact the matched value.

tools.purpose: Verify target GitHub App manifest permissions/events, no Actions write permission, registration form rendering, private manifest conversion, redacted summaries, and docs stay synchronized.
Verify GitHub App auth env parsing, JWT shape, installation-token caching, CLI profiles, GitHub Actions output masking, source invariants, and docs stay synchronized.
Verify GitHub App browser handoff routes are GET-only public-safe guidance surfaces that do not echo manifest codes, generated credentials, private repo details, or trigger review work.
Run deterministic no-network preflight fixtures for central App server and worker runtime postures.
Verify no-network preflight check order, strict/profile behavior, CLI flags, redacted diagnostics, and docs stay synchronized.
Verify the machine-readable v0 release gates match the release plan and that public evidence references point at existing docs or package scripts.
Verify v0 release-gate status readiness, missing-id checks, deferral semantics, Markdown redaction, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify saved webhook replay stays dry-run by default, requires explicit dispatch for worker queueing, signs payloads locally, avoids raw payload echo, and keeps replay docs synchronized.
Verify dogfood target packet modes, external config path redaction, Markdown sanitization, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify dogfood status readiness, missing-id checks, deferral semantics, Markdown redaction, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify dogfood readiness report defaults, operator workspace path redaction, preflight state, Markdown sanitization, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify dogfood promotion packet readiness gating, strict-preflight enforcement, workspace path redaction, Markdown sanitization, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify dogfood go-live packet readiness gating, strict-preflight enforcement, workspace path redaction, Markdown sanitization, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify operator workspace creation, community-release status, check-mode readiness failures, private path redaction, Markdown sanitization, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.

Verify the operator drill keeps temporary workspace cleanup, private path redaction, release-candidate, dogfood readiness, promotion, go-live summaries, production deployment plan handoff, next commands, and docs synchronized.

Verify operator evidence sections, readiness semantics, runtime dispatch evidence, container publish evidence, public dashboard disclosure evidence, private admin auth evidence, public-summary redaction, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify production cutover checklist/status readiness, deferral semantics, Markdown redaction, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify the dry-run production deployment plan keeps GitHub App, container publish, operator workspace, preflight, admin snapshot, cutover, and dogfood commands synchronized.
Verify the dry-run 6529.io dashboard deployment plan keeps frontend env, bot public disclosure, HMAC admin auth, dashboard verification, cutover evidence, and release notes synchronized.
Verify security review status readiness, deferral semantics, Markdown redaction, source invariants, and public docs stay synchronized.
Verify release-candidate bundle redaction, private workspace path markers, operator evidence section summaries, CLI flags, source invariants, and public release docs stay synchronized.

Verify the release notes draft command keeps candidate-bundle summaries, model catalog defaults, runtime dispatch evidence, container publish evidence, dashboard disclosure evidence, private admin auth evidence, alert evidence, TODO markers, redaction, CLI flags, source anchors, and docs synchronized.

Verify the release notes publication guard rejects unfinished TODO markers, Git ref-unsafe title versions, missing evidence fields, incomplete deferrals, vague or failed validation results, and public-safety leaks before tags or GitHub Releases.

Verify the release tag plan stays dry-run, checks clean main, local tag availability and remote tag availability, completed release notes, release notes title match, renders operator tag commands, and keeps public docs synchronized.
Validate public env templates for syntax, duplicate keys, blank secret placeholders, and conservative dogfood defaults.
Confirm committed workflow actions are pinned to reviewed commit SHAs.
Confirm committed workflow permission blocks stay least-privilege and avoid broad write scopes.
Validate the public/admin usage API OpenAPI contract consumed by 6529.io.

phases.title: Operator input preparation
phases.when: Run before live dogfood or production traffic, from an operator environment when private files or credentials are involved.
phases.boundary: Public repo carries templates and validators only. Live credentials, App ids, account ids, ARNs, private status files, and exact deployment targets stay outside the repo.

tools.purpose: Check central runtime configuration shape without network calls.
tools.privateInputs: Reads the current operator environment when run outside public examples.
tools.publicOutput: Share summaries only after reviewing warnings for private deployment details.

tools.purpose: Render and validate production-hosted GitHub App manifest settings without generating secrets.
tools.privateInputs: Production bot origin may be rollout-sensitive.
tools.publicOutput: Safe when hostnames are intended to be public.

tools.purpose: Exchange GitHub's one-hour manifest code and write generated credentials only to a private path.
tools.privateInputs: Manifest code, App id, client secret, webhook secret, and private key are private.
tools.publicOutput: Do not publish raw output paths or generated credential files.

tools.purpose: Render Aurora ledger schema SQL; add --apply only from the operator environment.
tools.privateInputs: Apply mode needs live AWS Data API settings.
tools.publicOutput: Schema text is public; apply evidence should be summarized.

tools.purpose: Validate central budget policy rows before optional operator apply.
tools.privateInputs: Real policy notes, requestor scopes, and rollout caps may be private.
tools.publicOutput: Share counts and high-level caps only when intended.

tools.purpose: Validate provider/model price rows, catalog default lane coverage, source URLs, and source-checked freshness before optional apply.
tools.privateInputs: Reviewed price files can include operator notes.
tools.publicOutput: Share provider/model coverage and freshness status, not private notes.

tools.purpose: Validate structured deployment evidence and render a redacted public summary.
tools.privateInputs: Private evidence may contain deployment details and internal evidence links.
tools.publicOutput: Only the redacted summary is intended for public release notes.

tools.purpose: Create the private release-gate, dogfood, security-review, cutover, and operator-evidence skeleton files in one workspace.
tools.privateInputs: Writes operator-owned private status and evidence files.
tools.publicOutput: Share only the public-safe summary, CLI counts, or release-candidate bundle output.

tools.purpose: Validate all current private workspace overlays together before final tag or traffic expansion decisions.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-owned private status and evidence files.
tools.publicOutput: Share only readiness counts or the release-candidate bundle output.

tools.purpose: Rehearse the release-candidate, production deployment plan, dogfood readiness, promotion, and go-live sequence against a private operator workspace and print public-safe next commands.
tools.privateInputs: Reads or writes operator-owned private workspace skeleton files.
tools.publicOutput: Share the public-safe report after reviewing command lines for private paths; prefer npm --silent run or --out with --quiet.

tools.purpose: Build the dry-run central App container publish plan before an operator builds, pushes, scans, and records image evidence.
tools.privateInputs: Reads local git state and may include an operator-owned registry or image repository in command output.
tools.publicOutput: Review before public sharing because command lines can include private registry names or deployment timing.

tools.purpose: Build the dry-run production deployment handoff plan across GitHub App registration, container publish, operator workspace, preflight, admin snapshot, cutover, and dogfood gates.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-supplied origins, registry names, and private workspace paths from command-line inputs.
tools.publicOutput: Review before public sharing because command lines can include private deployment origins, registry names, and workspace paths.

tools.purpose: Build the dry-run 6529.io dashboard deployment handoff plan across frontend env, bot public disclosure, HMAC admin auth, dashboard verification, cutover evidence, and release notes.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-supplied frontend origins, bot origins, auth-check URLs, and private workspace paths from command-line inputs.
tools.publicOutput: Review before public sharing because command lines can include private deployment origins, auth endpoint names, wallet-config context, and workspace paths.

tools.purpose: Build the dry-run production alert routing handoff plan across delivery mode configuration, private channel evidence, dry-run alert evaluation, admin status verification, cutover evidence, and release notes.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-supplied production origins, private workspace paths, notification modes, and private alert channel labels from command-line inputs.
tools.publicOutput: Review before public sharing because command lines can include private deployment origins, operator channel labels, alert destinations, and workspace paths.

phases.title: Dogfood execution
phases.when: Use for command-only and limited initial-review rollout on trusted repositories.
phases.boundary: Repository configs and public examples live here. Real dogfood status files, exact run keys, private payloads, and detailed operator evidence stay private.

tools.purpose: Validate the target repository config PR posture and render a public-safe PR checklist before opening the target repo PR.
tools.publicOutput: Safe when the repository name is intentionally public; external config paths are redacted.

tools.purpose: Replay synthetic self-dogfood payloads through the App pipeline to prove command-only behavior, deliberate multi-lane fanout, max-fanout rejection, and untrusted command denial without dispatching workers.
tools.publicOutput: Safe; uses synthetic public payload templates and dry-run queueing only.

tools.purpose: Check dogfood input files, model catalog, budget policy, repository config, private workspace parsing, and optional preflight before first traffic.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-owned private workspace files when --operator-workspace is supplied.
tools.publicOutput: Safe after reviewing preflight diagnostics and redacted workspace counts.

tools.purpose: Compose target config, central dogfood inputs, self-dogfood replay, private workspace parsing, and preflight into one final pre-traffic go/no-go packet.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-owned private workspace files and the private runtime environment when preflight is included.
tools.publicOutput: Designed to be public-safe after reviewing preflight diagnostics; use npm --silent run when private paths are in the command.

tools.purpose: Cross-check release-candidate, dogfood promotion, production cutover, and operator workspace evidence before command-only live dogfood traffic.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-owned private workspace files and private runtime preflight settings.
tools.publicOutput: Designed to be public-safe after reviewing preflight diagnostics; use npm --silent run when private paths are in the command.

tools.purpose: Create the private status skeleton for command-only and limited initial-review dogfood evidence.
tools.privateInputs: Writes an operator-owned private status file.
tools.publicOutput: Do not publish the raw private file.

tools.purpose: Fail expansion when current dogfood evidence is missing, pending, or blocked.
tools.privateInputs: Reads an operator-owned private status file.
tools.publicOutput: Share public-safe counts or release-candidate bundle output.

tools.purpose: Replay a saved GitHub webhook payload without dispatching workers.
tools.privateInputs: Payloads can include target repo metadata and user-controlled text.

phases.title: Manual security review
phases.when: Run before broad community use, and again whenever trust boundaries, credentials, cost controls, ledger writes, provider calls, or admin APIs change.
phases.boundary: The public checklist is committed. Reviewer notes, private payload examples, and exact deployment details remain in the private status overlay.

tools.purpose: Create a private status skeleton for manual security review evidence.
tools.privateInputs: Writes an operator-owned private status file.
tools.publicOutput: Do not publish the raw private file.

tools.purpose: Fail release prep when current manual security review evidence is missing, pending, or blocked.
tools.privateInputs: Reads an operator-owned private status file.
tools.publicOutput: Share public-safe counts or release-candidate bundle output.

tools.purpose: Render or run the crypto/security analysis prompt path for a target PR when local provider/GitHub settings are configured.
tools.privateInputs: Live runs can include PR diffs, source context, provider responses, and token usage.
tools.publicOutput: Only bot comments and redacted summaries should be public.

phases.title: Production cutover
phases.when: Use when enabling live dogfood or broader production traffic.
phases.boundary: The public checklist defines what must be proven. The private status overlay owns exact deployment targets, App ids, AWS identifiers, alert routing, and admin snapshots.

tools.purpose: Create the private status skeleton for production cutover evidence.
tools.privateInputs: Writes an operator-owned private status file.
tools.publicOutput: Do not publish the raw private file.

tools.purpose: Fail broad traffic expansion when current cutover evidence is missing, pending, or blocked.
tools.privateInputs: Reads an operator-owned private status file.
tools.publicOutput: Share public-safe counts or release-candidate bundle output.

tools.purpose: Check private admin API posture through the 6529.io auth bridge without exposing raw private rows.
tools.privateInputs: Requires private admin origin and auth bridge settings.
tools.publicOutput: Share endpoint counts and pass/fail posture only.

tools.purpose: Run scheduled budget and worker-health alert evaluation from the configured operator environment.
tools.privateInputs: Reads live ledger data and alert delivery settings when enabled.
tools.publicOutput: Share redacted alert posture, not raw delivery targets or payloads.

phases.title: Release candidate and tagging
phases.when: Use for release PRs, tag/no-tag decisions, and public release notes.
phases.boundary: The release-candidate command produces a public-safe bundle. Source evidence files remain private unless an operator intentionally publishes redacted excerpts.

tools.purpose: Validate the current v0 release-gate status overlay.
tools.privateInputs: Reads an operator-owned private gate status file.
tools.publicOutput: Share public-safe counts or release-candidate bundle output.

tools.purpose: Build the public-safe release-readiness bundle from a private operator workspace, git metadata, and preflight.
tools.privateInputs: Reads operator-owned private status and evidence files.
tools.publicOutput: Designed for public PRs, issues, releases, and durable manager memory after operator review.

tools.purpose: Build a public-safe pre-v1 release notes draft from a saved release-candidate JSON bundle and model catalog defaults.
tools.privateInputs: Reads a saved release-candidate JSON bundle; keep raw private status files out of this command.
tools.publicOutput: Designed as a first draft for public release notes after operator review and TODO completion.

tools.purpose: Check completed release notes and explicit validation evidence before publishing a tag or GitHub Release.
tools.privateInputs: Reads the operator-completed public release notes Markdown.
tools.publicOutput: Safe when the checked file is intended for publication.

tools.purpose: Build a dry-run tag plan that checks Git ref-safe tag names, clean synced main, completed release notes, release-note warnings, release notes title match, local tag availability, and remote tag availability, then prints the exact tag and GitHub Release steps for the operator.
tools.privateInputs: Reads local git state and the operator-completed public release notes Markdown.
tools.publicOutput: Safe when the checked release notes file is intended for publication and local paths are not included in copied command lines.

tools.purpose: Run the repository release gate used by CI before merging release-sensitive changes.
tools.publicOutput: Safe when failure diagnostics do not include private local details.

tools.purpose: Render this operations map for a quick command and evidence-boundary index.
Validate that the release notes template still names the required gates, evidence, deferrals, compatibility, and validation fields for pre-v1 releases.

phases.title: Support and incident response
phases.when: Use when an operator, maintainer, or community reporter needs triage evidence.
phases.boundary: Support artifacts are sanitized, but reports still need human review before public posting. Incident details can be sensitive even after redaction.

tools.purpose: Collect sanitized repository/runtime diagnostics for support triage.
tools.privateInputs: Can read local git/runtime posture.
tools.publicOutput: Review before posting because paths and feature posture may still be sensitive.

tools.purpose: Use preflight together with incident runbooks for containment and recovery posture checks.
tools.privateInputs: Reads the current operator environment when configured.
